author: Appraises
text:
Gate non-default state_branch as unsupported

bl-8a9a resolved (tracker_address) and materialized (state_repo) the SPEC-tracker-state §5 state_branch field, but every claim/review/close/sync push/fetch/merge path still hardcodes git_state::STATE_BRANCH ("balls/tasks"). A non-default value checks a mismatched branch out in .balls/state-repo while all state traffic targets balls/tasks — a silently half-working field.

This audit child verifies and gates: tracker_address::ensure_supported hard-errors a non-default state_branch with a clear hand-edit recovery message, and Config::validate calls it so the gate fronts every command. The field has no CLI writer today, so a hand-edit is the only way in — and the only way out. Honest 'not yet' over silent misroute.

Full plumbing (thread the resolved branch through the traffic, add bl remaster --branch per SPEC §8, add a SPEC §16 conformance test, drop the gate) is filed as follow-up bl-3f59. A pre-existing tarpaulin-timing flake surfaced while running the coverage gate, filed as bl-0ca1.
